skill.name: cold-chain-risk-calculator
skill.description: Calculate temperature excursion risks for cold chain transport. Assesses route risk, packaging suitability, and monitoring requirements for biological samples and pharmaceuticals requiring controlled-temperature shipping.
skill.summary: Assess temperature excursion risk for cold chain transport routes with duration and packaging analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 56 static analysis findings are false positives. The 23 weak cryptographic algorithm detections target JSON field labels, markdown headings, and Python argparse code that contain no cryptographic material. The 31 Ruby/shell backtick execution detections are markdown fenced code blocks showing legitimate Python usage examples. The 2 hardcoded URL and 3 system reconnaissance detections point to a GitHub source attribution link and informational text. This is a legitimate cold chain risk calculation utility with no security threats.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm misdetection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 23 instances of weak cryptographic algorithms across JSON, Python, and markdown files. All flagged lines contain non-cryptographic content such as JSON field labels (score, PASS, result), Python argparse initialization, and markdown documentation text. No cryptographic algorithms are present in the skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 31 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. All instances are markdown fenced code blocks using triple backticks for documentation. These are safe documentation examples, not executable shell commands or Ruby backtick operators.

low_findings.title: Source URL misidentified as hardcoded network endpoint
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL as a hardcoded network endpoint. This is a source attribution link in the skill documentation, not a malicious network call or data exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: Informational text misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged informational documentation text and JSON audit result fields as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate descriptions of the skill's use cases and error handling procedures.

content.user_title: Calculate cold chain transport risk scores
content.value_statement: Cold chain risk calculator helps logistics teams assess temperature excursion risk for biological samples and pharmaceuticals before shipping. It evaluates route duration and packaging type to produce structured risk scores with mitigation recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Calculate risk scores for cold chain transport routes using duration and packaging type inputs
Classify risk levels as Low, Medium, or High based on a documented scoring formula
Generate mitigation recommendations for temperature-sensitive biological and pharmaceutical shipments
Support three packaging types: dry ice, liquid nitrogen, and gel packs with distinct risk factors
Reject invalid parameters such as negative duration or unsupported packaging values
Produce structured JSON output with risk score, risk level, and actionable recommendations
limitations: Does not model route complexity such as number of transit legs or transfer points
Cannot track shipments in real time or provide live temperature monitoring
Does not account for ambient temperature variability across different geographic regions
Not suitable for clinical diagnostics, drug dosing calculations, or non-temperature logistics

use_cases.title: Pre-shipment risk assessment for biological samples
use_cases.description: A lab manager evaluates the risk of shipping frozen biological samples from New York to Boston using dry ice packaging over 48 hours.

use_cases.title: Packaging selection for pharmaceutical logistics
use_cases.description: A logistics coordinator compares risk scores across dry ice, liquid nitrogen, and gel packs to select optimal packaging for a 72-hour international pharmaceutical shipment.

use_cases.title: Regulatory risk documentation for quality assurance
use_cases.description: A QA officer generates structured risk reports with documented assumptions and mitigation recommendations for regulatory compliance review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic cold chain risk check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cold-chain-risk-calculator to assess risk for shipping samples from Chicago to Detroit. Duration is 24 hours. Packaging is dry ice.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple route assessment with all required inputs available

prompt_templates.title: Risk comparison across packaging types
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to ship vaccines from Los Angeles to London in 120 hours. Compare the risk scores for dry ice, liquid nitrogen, and gel packs. Recommend the best option and explain the trade-offs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting optimal packaging based on risk comparison

prompt_templates.title: Risk assessment with documentation requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a cold chain risk assessment for a 96-hour shipment from Tokyo to Singapore using gel packs. Provide the full structured output including risk score, risk level, and mitigation recommendations. Document all assumptions about ambient temperature and route complexity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating documented risk report for quality or regulatory review

prompt_templates.title: Partial input handling with fallback reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to assess cold chain risk for a shipment but I only know the route is NYC to Berlin and the packaging is liquid nitrogen. The duration is unknown. Use the fallback approach to determine what partial assessment is still possible and what additional input is needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Incomplete inputs with fallback manual risk-scoring approach

output_examples.input: Assess risk for shipping samples from NYC to Boston. Duration is 48 hours. Packaging is dry ice.
output_examples.output: The assessment shows a risk score of 19.2 with a Medium risk level. Recommended mitigations include adding a temperature logger to the shipment, pre-conditioning dry ice two hours before packing, and notifying the recipient of the expected arrival window.

output_examples.input: Compare dry ice and gel packs for a 72-hour vaccine shipment from Miami to Atlanta.
output_examples.output: Dry ice produces a risk score of 28.8 which is Medium risk. Gel packs produce a risk score of 43.2 which is High risk. Dry ice is recommended for this route. Important limitation: the model does not account for ambient temperature differences between the two cities.

best_practices: Always specify the packaging type explicitly to ensure accurate risk factor application in the calculation.
Document all assumptions about ambient temperature and route complexity in every response to maintain transparency.
Use the structured output format for integration with reporting systems and regulatory documentation workflows.
anti_patterns: Using the tool for real-time shipment tracking instead of pre-shipment risk assessment planning.
Applying the risk model to non-temperature-sensitive shipments or general logistics scenarios.
Ignoring documented model limitations about route complexity when making critical shipping decisions.

faq.question: What inputs does the cold chain risk calculator require?
faq.answer: The tool requires a route description, transport duration in hours, and an optional packaging type. Duration must be a positive integer. Packaging defaults to dry ice if not specified.

faq.question: How is the risk score calculated?
faq.answer: Risk score equals duration in hours times 0.5 times the packaging factor. Packaging factors are 0.8 for dry ice, 0.6 for liquid nitrogen, and 1.2 for gel packs.

faq.question: What risk levels does this tool produce?
faq.answer: Three risk levels are used: Low for scores below 15, Medium for scores between 15 and 30, and High for scores above 30.

faq.question: Can I use this tool for real-time shipment tracking?
faq.answer: No. This tool is designed for pre-shipment risk assessment only. It does not support real-time tracking, live monitoring, or dynamic route updates.

faq.question: What packaging types are supported?
faq.answer: Three packaging types are supported: dry ice for standard minus 70 degree Celsius samples, liquid nitrogen for cryogenic samples, and gel packs for 2 to 8 degree Celsius shipments.

faq.question: Does the model account for route complexity or weather conditions?
faq.answer: No. The current model uses only duration and packaging type. It does not consider number of transit legs, geographic complexity, or ambient temperature variability.
